claim: Breed explains only 9% of the variation in individual dog behavior across 18,385 dogs studied via GWAS and owner surveys.
stance_target: breed-based behavioral prediction as a reliable method for individual dogs
scope_conditions: 2,155 genome-sequenced dogs + 18,385 surveyed; 78 breeds via Darwin's Ark database; published Science 2022
quoted_support: They found breed explains only about 9 percent of the variation in an individual dogs' behavior. 'It's a major advance in how we think about dog behavior. No breed owns any particular trait.'

claim: Some behavioral tendencies correlate with breed at the population level (Border collies more responsive to direction; German shorthaired pointers more likely to point), but these are probabilistic nudges, not destinies.
stance_target: partial validity of breed priors at population level
scope_conditions: Morrill et al. 2022 GWAS study; behavioral traits assessed via owner surveys
quoted_support: 'Genetics matter, but genetics are a nudge in a given direction. They're not a destiny,' Evan MacLean, director of the Arizona Canine Cognition Center, tells NPR.

claim: No behavioral trait was present in all dogs of a breed or absent from all dogs of a breed in the Morrill et al. 2022 study.
scope_conditions: 18,385 owner surveys + genome sequencing; Darwin's Ark data
quoted_support: Researchers found no behavioral trait was present in all dogs in a breed or missing from all of them either.
